mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate signals ongoing discount-rate support for equities, so when FEDFUNDS stays near the zero lower bound the S&P 500 should post positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if rate stability near zero does not coincide with above-baseline equity gains.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS stays below 0.50% but 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of such observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates tightening labor-market stress and weaker household income growth, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when unemployment accelerates rather than merely stays high.
failure_condition: If triggered episodes with UNRATE rising more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months still produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in housing starts and retail sales indicates broadening domestic demand that can lift earnings expectations and support equity prices over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is not separable: both HOUST and RSAFS must improve together to validate the signal.
failure_condition: Reject if joint upticks in HOUST and RSAFS do not outperform single-series signals, or if the combined condition fails to yield positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production together with falling unemployment indicates synchronized real-sector expansion, which should translate into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when output growth is not offset by labor weakness.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: If the joint condition of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE does not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the hit rate is below 55%, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A broad-based housing downturn, captured by HOUST falling below its recent level, signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and slower growth expectations, which should weigh on equities over the next 60 trading days. The effect fails when housing declines are isolated and not accompanied by broader macro softening.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below its 6-month moving average is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation rises while the policy rate remains unchanged, real rates fall and financial conditions ease, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is a short-horizon repricing effect and should fail when inflation rises in tandem with policy tightening.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS is unchanged over the prior month
failure_condition: If CPI inflation accelerations with unchanged FEDFUNDS do not deliver positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if returns are negative in a majority of triggered instances, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in PAYEMS together with a falling UNRATE indicates durable payroll expansion and broad labor-market improvement, which should lift equity returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger income and consumption expectations. The signal fails if payroll gains are concentrated in weak-demand periods that do not reduce unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS gains and UNRATE declines do not outperform either proxy alone, or if positive 60-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of matched observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary demand shock, weak retail sales together with falling industrial production indicate synchronized household and business retrenchment, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is specific to downturn regimes and should not be applied when one series improves strongly.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines and INDPRO declines over the prior 2 months
failure_condition: If joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or if the conditional mean return is not negative, reject the hypothesis.
